RT by @no_itsmyturn: MORE: At least seven vessels have changed course to avoid transiting through the Bab al Mandeb Strait in light of the Houthi blockade on Saudi ports. The Houthis imposed the blockade against Saudi Arabia on July 20. Six commercial vessels altered their course away from Saudi ports on July 22, according to ship-tracking data cited by Reuters. Another oil tanker that was bound for Yanbu — Saudi Arabia’s main oil export terminal on the Red Sea — turned away from the Bab al Mandeb Strait while in the Gulf of Aden on July 22. The European Union Naval Force warned commercial vessels with links to the United States, Israel, and Saudi Arabia on July 22 against sailing in the Red Sea and Gulf of Aden. Commercial ship traffic through the Bab al Mandeb Strait continued on July 22 despite the Houthis’ threats against commercial vessels, however. Commercially available ship tracking data from July 22 shows that 30 commercial vessels have transited through the Bab al Mandab Strait since ISW-CTP’s last data cutoff on July 21. This is a notable decrease from the 64 commercial vessels that transited the strait between 2:00 PM ET on July 20 and 2:00 PM ET on July 21, however. At least one oil tanker transporting crude oil from Yanbu, the Xin Long Yang, initially changed course on July 21 to avoid crossing the Bab al Mandeb Strait but has begun to reapproach the strait on July 22. Houthi officials have emphasized that the Houthis will maintain their blockade on Saudi ports until Saudi Arabia lifts its blockade on Yemeni ports and airports. ISW-CTP will provide further analysis about the Houthi blockade against Saudi Arabia in its July 23 Iran Update.

Al Jazeera: "Most exports from Yanbu [Saudi Arabia] are loaded onto very large crude carriers (VLCCs), but fully laden VLCCs cannot pass through the Suez Canal because they sit too deep in the water. Operators instead would either have to transfer part of the cargo before reaching the canal using Egypt’s SUMED pipeline or switch to smaller Suezmax tankers that can transit the canal more easily. Both options would add time and costs." https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/7/22/can-the-suez-save-asian-oil-consumers-after-houthis-shut-bab-al-mandeb

RT by @IntelDoge: Ukraine continued its ongoing campaign to strike tankers in the Sea of #Azov and the #BlackSea over the week. Multiple tankers were successfully struck by Ukrainian drones. These tankers are part of the Russian shadow fleet which is used to cirumvent sanctions.

RT by @IntelDoge: Drones struck the warehouse and set it on fire which resulted in a massive smoke plume that was very easily visible from space and across the southern sides of the Moscow suburbs. This warehouse served as a logistics hub helping to support the Russian war effort.
RT by @IntelDoge: Ukrainian forces launched a large scale drone attack on a massive #Wildberries warehouse outside of #Moscow, Russia which resulted in the complete destruction of the building.

RT by @IntelDoge: This attack came as Ukrainian forces are increasingly short on interceptor missiles which has resulted in a significant increase in the number of missiles getting through and striking their targets.
RT by @IntelDoge: Air attacks continued throughout the week while a massive ballistic missile attack took place on Saturday. 41 missiles targeted #Kyiv during the attack.

RT by @IntelDoge: Popular opinion both with citizens and with those who have or are actively enlisted in the Ukrainian military is largely negative of Syrskyi, and the dismissal of Fedorov this week has only reinforced that view. Russia did not slow down its attacks on Ukraine this week.
RT by @IntelDoge: The relatively sudden dismissal comes as a clear rift between #Fedorov and General #Syrskyi, who is in command of the #UkrainianArmedForces, came to a head.

RT by @IntelDoge: #Ukraine War Update July 18th, 2026 It has been an unsettled week in Ukraine following the ouster of Defense Minister Mykhailo Fedorov. Protests in support of Fedorov took place across cities in Ukraine and public figures spoke out in support of the former Defense Minister.
